summary: Excellent 1967 soundboard recording of a high‑energy show highlighted by a historic first “The Other One” and crisp, clean remastering.
review: This Miller soundboard capture delivers astonishing clarity for a ’67 performance – the mix is described as crisp, clean and deliciously detailed. Musically the concert shines with the debut of The Other One (praised as “ridiculous” and “smoking”), a soaring Lovelight, and a fifteen‑minute “That’s It” that many fans call an orgasmic climax. Jerry Garcia’s lead work is repeatedly called scorching, while Pigpen’s organ on The Other One earns top marks.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (average ≈ 4.85) and uniformly enthusiastic comments about both performance and the remastered soundboard indicate an outstanding show with excellent recording quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers repeatedly note the recording is crisp, clean, deliciously detailed and surprisingly vivid for a 1967 source; no significant drop‑outs or balance issues reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy flow with dynamic rhythms; the band rolls from laid‑back moments into extended jams, especially The Other One and That’s It, keeping listeners fully engaged.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is repeatedly called “shredding”, “smoking hot” and delivering scorching lead guitar throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen's organ on The Other One is highlighted as top‑notch, underscoring the early Anthem era keyboard work.
